stimulus: The Mariner, Viking, and Pathfinder missions to Mars provided early estimates of the age of the planet’s surface based on rock and dust samples surveyed at their landing sites. More recently, NASA’s Perseverance rover provided additional samples from a different region of Mars. Geologists have analyzed and dated each of the samples, concluding that the samples surveyed by Perseverance are significant because ______
stem: Which choice most effectively uses data from the table to complete the claim?

answerOptions.content: they were collected from the same region as Mariner 9.
they are much younger than the samples surveyed by any of the earlier missions.
they are closest in age to the samples surveyed by Viking 1.
they helped confirm the predicted ages of the samples from the earlier missions.

rationale: Choice B is the best answer because the table shows Perseverance samples are about 250 million years old, while every earlier mission’s samples exceed 1,000 million years — Perseverance’s samples are much younger than any earlier mission’s. Choice A is incorrect because Perseverance surveyed Jezero Crater, not Mariner 9’s Valles Marineris. Choice C is incorrect because Viking 1’s samples (1,500) are far from 250; the closest in age would be Mariner 9. Choice D is incorrect because nothing in the table or text suggests Perseverance confirmed predicted ages.
